title: How to contribute to Rgemini
text: welcome contributions Rgemini GEMINI team members. submit suggestions bug fixes/enhancements, please create new issue providing sufficient details bug report/feature request.

title: How to contribute to Rgemini
text: make changes Rgemini codebase/documentation, please follow steps guidelines: Create new issue assign existing issue want work . Create new branch develop. branch name contain issue number. Commit changes newly created branch include issue number commit message. Run styler linter new code using styler:::style_active_file() lintr::lint("path//file.R"). Make sure using lintr version 3.0.0 newer compatibility package .lintr file. appropriate, add unit tests tests/testthat/ directory. bug fixes, ’s usually helpful include unit test identified bug. Note CI/CD workflow automatically runs unit tests R Python (via rpy2) ensure cross-language compatibility. Python-specific debugging development, see Python Testing Workflow guide. adding new functions, update _pkgdown.yml file adding new function appropriate reference section. Documentation functions generated automatically CI/CD workflow, devtools::document() can also run development debug locally. adding new package dependencies: First check whether existing dependencies listed (DESCRIPTION.md)[https://github.com/GEMINI-Medicine/Rgemini/blob/main/DESCRIPTION] achieve similar task (want try minimize package dependencies possible). require new library, please add Imports section (DESCRIPTION.md)[https://github.com/GEMINI-Medicine/Rgemini/blob/main/DESCRIPTION]. package strictly necessary run Rgemini functions, used illustration purposes vignettes, please list Suggests. Functions derived based published articles (e.g. clinical scores) include references section roxygen. citation format : 1st_author_last_name 1st_author_initial, et al. Journal Abbreviation, Year. https://doi.org/xxxx.. URLs presented -without hyperlinking DOI URLs used whenever possible. Note commonly known online resources (e.g. linked CIHI documentation, data dictionary) might require references section can included hyperlinks roxygen section. new package data saved .rda file data/ directory, documented roxygen data.R. finish code development relevant documentation, add new item top NEWS.md concisely describing ’s changed. Submit pull request (PR) develop branch. trigger CI/CD pipeline ) styles code files generates roxygen documentation man folder, b) runs pre-commit hooks check browser() statements large files, c) checks unit tests (R Python), d) runs full R-CMD package checks (.e., devtools::check()). Note run cross-platform R-CMD checks Windos, macOS, Ubuntu since users might use certain functions (especially utility functions/functions don’t require database connection) different operating systems. Code developers responsible resolving CI/CD errors merging code. Ask team member review pull request (see guidelines reviewers ) implement additional changes based reviewer’s feedback. reviewer approved pull request, resolve merge conflicts double check CI/CD workflow make sure runs without errors. Finally, squash commits (confirm PR # referenced squash commit message) merge branch develop, close issue, delete branch developed .

title: How to contribute to Rgemini
text: pull requests (PRs) carefully reviewed least one person. detailed instructions look review, please refer Code Review Checklist. Briefly, reviewing code: Assign reviewer PR (go “Pull requests” -> click PR -> click “assign ” “Reviewers”). Pull updated code branch associated issue. can also install package specific branch using remotes::install_github("GEMINI-Medicine/Rgemini@<branch_name>") Make sure can run code without error messages. Check produces expected outcome resolves issue. possible, review line code changed/added provide feedback anything think improved. comment individual lines code, go “Pull requests” -> click PR -> “Files changed” -> + add comments. can also share general feedback submit review, use “Conversation” section PR discuss open questions developer throughout review process. Please share feedback GitHub (instead slack/email) transparency make sure previous discussions well documented. reviewer, usually push changes code , instead, mention suggestions review developer responsible implementing changes. developer addressed comments, approve pull request (developer merge PR close issue).

title: How to contribute to Rgemini
text: typically accumulate multiple changes develop branch. package owner merge changes main update package version number. package owner always review pull request main run following final checks approval: Check whether changes main yet develop. yes, merge main develop. Make sure changes develop summarized NEWS.md. new functions added, make sure listed _pkgdown.yml. Decide new version number based guidelines . Update version number NEWS.md DESCRIPTION files. Running devtools::document() rcmdcheck::rcmdcheck() optional since already done part CI/CD workflow. everything looks good, approve pull request merge develop main (without squashing commits). Add new tag repository corresponding updated version number. Notify team updated version. Ideally, members immediately update newest version Rgemini. Update HPC4Health users: Download tar.gz file newest package version save R:/GEMINI/HPC4Health/Rgemini versions/. Submit request HPC4Health File Transfer Log package file transferred /mnt/nfs/pkgs/GEMINI/. Ask systems team install updated package globally users send email HPC4Health users announcing new release.

title: Authors and Citation
text: GEMINI. Maintainer.

title: Authors and Citation
text: GEMINI (2026). gemSim: GEMINI Data Simulator. R package version 1.0.0, https://gemini-medicine.github.io/gemSim/.
code: @Manual{,   title = {gemSim: GEMINI Data Simulator},   author = {{GEMINI}},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 1.0.0},   url = {https://gemini-medicine.github.io/gemSim/}, }

title: GEMINI Data Simulator
text: gemSim custom R package generating synthetic datasets mimic structure GEMINI database. package sample reproduce real GEMINI data. Instead, generates fully synthetic, randomly simulated data designed approximate database schema (e.g., table names key linkage variables) high-level distributional characteristics GEMINI’s current data holdings (e.g., age & gender distributions, frequency lab/radiology testing, hospital-level variability etc.). data elements simulated ways produce plausible individual records. instance, clinical date-time variables generated fall within admission–discharge window given hospital encounter. However, many aspects data intended reflect real clinical relationships. particular, clinical outcomes, predictors, covariates commonly used research simulated independently one another therefore interpreted meaningful associations. result, primary purpose gemSim support software development testing without requiring access real GEMINI data. includes, limited :: Unit testing within CI/CD pipelines (e.g., Rgemini) Debugging analytics reporting workflows Demonstrations example code (e.g., package vignettes) Development new utility functions

title: GEMINI Data Simulator
text: gemSim currently creates synthetic data following tables (see GEMINI data dictionary details): Admdad Locality Variables IPSCU ER IP Diagnosis ER Diagnosis IP Intervention (MRI MAID) ER Intervention (MRI) Lab (CBC electrolyte) Radiology Transfusion Physicians

title: GEMINI Data Simulator
text: gemSim currently available CRAN. Instead, please install package directly GitHub follows: Alternatively, can git clone gemSim repository main branch, run devtools::install("/path//repo", repos = NULL, type="source").
code: remotes::install_github("GEMINI-Medicine/gemSim", dependencies = TRUE)

title: GEMINI Data Simulator
text: simulate data single table, run: simulate multiple tables maintaining key inter-table relationships (e.g., genc_ids, hospital_nums, plausible date-times), use wrapper function simulate_data_tables():
code: library(gemSim) admdad <- dummy_admdad(   nid = 1000, # number of encounters to be simulated   n_hospitals = 10, # number of hospitals to be simulated   time_period = c(2015, 2023), # fiscal years to be simulated   seed = 1 # seed for reproducilibity ) dummy_data <- simulate_data_tables(   tables = c(     "admdad", "ipscu", "er", "erdiagnosis", "ipdiagnosis", "locality_variables",     "lab","radiology", "erintervention", "ipintervention", "transfusion",     "physicians"   ),   nid = 5000,   time_period = c(2021, 2024),   seed = 1 )

title: GEMINI Data Simulator
text: Using gemSim package, dummy GEMINI database 5,000 randomly simulated genc_ids created, can queried follows: following code used generate dummy DB:
code: library(DBI) library(RPostgres) library(data.table)  # connect to dummy DB con <- dbConnect(   RPostgres::Postgres(),   host     = "gemini-db-dummy.j.aivencloud.com",   port     = 10571,   dbname   = "dummy_db_v1_0_0", # most recent version   user     = "gemini_user",   password = "gemini",   sslmode  = "require" )  # query dummy admdad table adm <- dbGetQuery(con, "SELECT * FROM admdad;") library(gemSim)  test_data <- simulate_data_tables(   c("admdad", "ipscu", "er", "erdiagnosis", "ipdiagnosis", "locality_variables",     "lab","radiology", "erintervention", "ipintervention", "transfusion",     "physicians"), nid = 5000, time_period = c(2021, 2024), seed = 1)

title: Simulate admdad data — dummy_admdad
text: function creates dummy dataset subset variables contained GEMINI "admdad" table (see details GEMINI Data Repository Dictionary). simulated encounter-level variables returned function currently: Admission date-time, discharge date-time, age, gender, discharge disposition, transfer alternate level care (ALC), ALC days. distribution simulated variables roughly mimics real distribution variable observed GIM cohort 2015-2022. Admission date-time simulated conjunction discharge date-time mimic realistic length stay. variables simulated independently , .e., correlation age, gender, discharge disposition etc. may exist real data. One exception number_of_alc_days, > 0 entries alc_service_transfer_flag == TRUE length ALC capped total length stay. function simulates patient populations differ across hospitals. , patient characteristics simulated separately hospital, different, randomly drawn distribution mean (.e., random intercepts). However, degree hospital-level variation simulated function arbitrary reflect true differences hospitals real GEMINI dataset.

title: Simulate admdad data — dummy_admdad
code: dummy_admdad(   nid = 1000,   n_hospitals = 10,   time_period = c(2015, 2023),   seed = NULL )

title: Simulate admdad data — dummy_admdad
text: nid (integer) Total number encounters (genc_ids) simulated. n_hospitals (integer) Number hospitals simulated. Total number genc_ids split pseudo-randomly hospitals ensure roughly equal sample size hospital. time_period (vector) numeric vector containing time period, specified fiscal years (starting April year). example, c(2015, 2019) generates data 2015-04-01 2020-03-31. seed (numeric) Optional, number set seed reproducible results

title: Simulate admdad data — dummy_admdad
text: (data.frame) data.frame object similar "admdad" table containing following fields: genc_id (integer): Mock encounter ID; integers starting 1 hospital_num (integer): Mock hospital ID number; integers starting 1 admission_date_time (character): Date-time admission YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM format discharge_date_time (character): Date-time discharge YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM format age (integer): Patient age gender (character): Patient gender (F/M/O Female/Male/) discharge_disposition (integer): valid categories according DAD abstracting manual 2022-2023 4: Home Support/Referral 5: Private Home 8: Cadaveric Donor (exist GEMINI data) 9: Stillbirth (exist GEMINI data) 10: Transfer Inpatient Care 20: Transfer ED Ambulatory Care 30: Transfer Residential Care 40: Transfer Group/Supportive Living 90: Transfer Correctional Facility 61: Absent Without Leave (AWOL) 62: Left Medical Advice (LAMA) 65: Return Pass/Leave 66: Died Pass/Leave 67: Suicide Facility (exist GEMINI data) 72: Died Facility 73: Medical Assistance Dying (MAID) 74: Suicide Facility alc_service_transfer_flag (character): Variable indicating whether patient transferred alternate level care (ALC) hospital stay. Coding messy varies across sites. Possible values : Missing: NA, "" True: "TRUE"/"true"/"T", "y"/"Y", "1"/"99", "ALC" False: "FALSE"/"false", "N", "0", "non-ALC" entries missing alc_service_transfer_flag can inferred based value number_of_alc_days (see ) number_of_alc_days (integer): Number days spent ALC (rounded nearest integer). number_of_alc_days = 0, ALC occurred; number_of_alc_days > 0, ALC occurred. Note days spent ALC usually < length stay. However, due fact ALC days rounded , possible number_of_alc_days larger los_days_derived.

title: Simulate admdad data — dummy_admdad
code: # Simulate 10,000 encounters from 10 hospitals for fiscal years 2018-2020. admdad <- dummy_admdad(nid = 10000, n_hospitals = 10, time_period = c(2018, 2020))

what: Generate Simulated Diagnosis Data Table — dummy_diag
title: Generate Simulated Diagnosis Data Table — dummy_diag
text: function generates simulated data table resembling ipdiagnosis erdiagnosis tables can used testing demonstration purposes. internally calls sample_icd() function sample ICD-10 codes accepts arguments passed sample_icd() customizing sampling scheme.

title: Generate Simulated Diagnosis Data Table — dummy_diag
code: dummy_diag(   nid = 1000,   n_hospitals = 10,   cohort = NULL,   ipdiagnosis = TRUE,   diagnosis_type = NULL,   seed = NULL,   ... )

title: Generate Simulated Diagnosis Data Table — dummy_diag
text: nid (integer) Number unique encounter IDs (genc_id) simulate. Value must greater 0. Optional cohort provided. n_hospitals (integer) Number hospitals simulate resulting data table. Optional cohort provided. cohort (data.frame data.table) Optional, administrative data frame columns: genc_id (integer): GEMINI encounter ID hospital_num (integer): hospital ID numbers cohort takes precedence parameters nid andn_hospitals. cohort NULL, nid n_hospitals ignored. ipdiagnosis (logical) Default "TRUE" returns simulated "ipdiagnosis" table. FALSE, returns simulated "erdiagnosis" table. See tables GEMINI Data Repository Dictionary. diagnosis_type (character vector) type(s) diagnosis return. Possible diagnosis types ("M", 1", "2", "3", "4", "5", "6", "9", "W", "X", "Y"). Regardless diagnosis_type input, ipdiagnosis table defaulted always return type "M" first row encounter. seed (integer) Optional, number assign seed . ... Additional arguments ICD code sampling scheme. See sample_icd() details.

title: Generate Simulated Diagnosis Data Table — dummy_diag
text: (data.table) data table containing simulated data genc_id, (er)_diagnosis_code, (er)_diagnosis_type, hospital_num, fields found respective diagnosis table.

title: Generate Simulated Diagnosis Data Table — dummy_diag
text: ensure simulated table resembles "ip(er)diagnosis" table, following characteristics applied fields: genc_id: Numerical identification encounters starting 1. number unique encounters defined n. total number rows defined nrow, number rows encounter random, encounter least one row. hospital_num: Numerical identification hospitals 1 5. rows encounter linked single hospital diagnosis_code: "ipdiagnosis" table . Simulated ICD-10 diagnosis codes. encounter can associated multiple diagnosis codes long format. diagnosis_type: "ipdiagnosis" table . first row encounter consistently assigned diagnosis type "M". remaining rows, diagnosis_type specified users, diagnosis types sampled randomly values provided; diagnosis_type NULL, diagnosis types sampled ("1", "2", "3", "4", "5", "6", "9", "W", "X", "Y"), sampling probability proportionate prevalence "ipdiagnosis" table. diagnosis_cluster: "ipdiagnosis" table . Proportionally sampled values prevalence 1% "diagnosis_cluster" field "ipdiagnosis" table, ("", "", "B"). diagnosis_prefix: "ipdiagnosis" table . Proportionally sampled values prevalence 1% "diagnosis_prefix" field "ipdiagnosis" table, ("", "N", "Q", "6"). er_diagnosis_code: "erdiagnosis" table . Simulated ICD-10 diagnosis codes. encounter can associated multiple diagnosis codes long format. er_diagnosis_type: "erdiagnosis" table . Proportionally sampled values prevalence 1% "er_diagnosis_type" field "erdiagnosis" table, ("", "M", "9", "3", "O").

title: Generate Simulated Diagnosis Data Table — dummy_diag
text: following fields (er)diagnosis_code, (er)diagnosis_type, diagnosis_cluster, diagnosis_prefix simulated independently. Therefore, simulated combinations may reflect interrelationships fields actual data. example, specific diagnosis codes may associated specific diagnosis types, diagnosis clusters, diagnosis prefix reality. However, relationships maintained purpose generating dummy data. Users require specific linkages fields consider customizing output data manually generating desired combinations.

title: Generate Simulated Diagnosis Data Table — dummy_diag
code: ### Simulate an erdiagnosis table for 5 unique subjects with total 20 records: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ set.seed(1) erdiag <- dummy_diag(nid = 50, n_hospitals = 2, ipdiagnosis = F) } # }  ### Simulate an erdiagnosis table including data from `cohort` cohort <- dummy_admdad() erdiag <- dummy_diag(cohort = cohort)  ### Simulate an ipdiagnosis table with diagnosis codes starting with "E11": if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ set.seed(1) ipdiag <- dummy_diag(nid = 50, n_hospitals = 20, ipdiagnosis = T, pattern = "^E11") } # }  ### Simulate a ipdiagnosis table with random diagnosis codes in diagnosis type 3 or 6 only: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ set.seed(1) ipdiag <- dummy_diag(nid = 50, n_hospitals = 10, diagnosis_type = (c("3", "6"))) %>%   filter(diagnosis_type != "M") # remove default rows with diagnosis_type="M" from each ID } # }  ### Simulate a ipdiagnosis table with ICD-10-CA codes: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ drv <- dbDriver("PostgreSQL") dbcon <- DBI::dbConnect(drv,   dbname = "db",   host = "172.XX.XX.XXX",   port = 1234,   user = getPass("Enter user:"),   password = getPass("password") )  set.seed(1) ipdiag <- dummy_diag(nid = 5, n_hospitals = 2, ipdiagnosis = T, dbcon = dbcon, source = "icd_lookup") } # }

title: Generate simulated ER data. — dummy_er
text: function creates dummy dataset subset variables contained GEMINI "er" table, seen GEMINI Data Repository Dictionary. function return one triage date time encounter ID.

title: Generate simulated ER data. — dummy_er
code: dummy_er(   nid = 1000,   n_hospitals = 10,   time_period = c(2015, 2023),   cohort = NULL,   seed = NULL )

title: Generate simulated ER data. — dummy_er
text: nid (integer) Number unique encounter IDs simulate. data table, ID occurs . Optional cohort provided. n_hospitals (integer) Number hospitals simulated dataset. Optional cohort provided. time_period (vector) numeric character vector containing data range data years specific dates either format: ("yyyy-mm-dd", "yyyy-mm-dd") (yyyy, yyyy). start date end date (yyyy-01-01 yyyy-12-31) (yyyy, yyyy) date range format provided. Optional cohort provided. cohort (data.frame data.table): Optional, data frame following columns: genc_id (integer): Mock encounter ID hospital_num (integer): Mock hospital ID number admission_date_time (character): date time admission hospital format "%Y-%m-%d %H:%M" discharge_date_time (character): date time discharge hospital format "%Y-%m-%d %H:%M" cohort NULL, nid, n_hospitals, time_period ignored. seed (integer) Optional, number setting seed get reproducible results.

title: Generate simulated ER data. — dummy_er
text: (data.table) data.table object similar "er" table contains following fields: genc_id (integer): Mock encounter ID; integers starting 1 cohort provided hospital_num (integer): Mock hospital ID; integers starting 1 cohort provided triage_date_time (character): date time triage format "%Y-%m-%d %H:%M"

text: nid (integer) Number unique encounter IDs simulate. Encounter IDs may repeat, resulting data table rows nid. Ignored cohort provided. n_hospitals (integer) Number hospitals simulate. Ignored cohort provided. int_code (character vector) Optional, user-specified intervention codes include returned data table. needs valid MRI code. cohort (data.frame data.table) Optional, data frame data table containing fields: genc_id (integer): Mock encounter ID number hospital_num (integer): Mock hospital ID number cohort NULL, nid n_hospitals ignored. seed (integer) Optional, number used set seed reproducible results

title: Generate simulated erintervention data — dummy_erintervention_mri
text: (data.table) data.table object similar "ipintervention" table contains columns: genc_id (integer): Mock encounter ID number; integers starting 1 cohort hospital_num (integer): Mock hospital ID number; integers starting 1 cohort intervention_code (character): Valid CCI code(s) describing services (procedures/intervention) performed behalf patient improve health. simulation, MRI.

title: Generate simulated ipscu data. — dummy_ipscu
text: nid (integer) Number unique encounter IDs simulate. Encounter IDs may repeat due repeat visits simulated, resulting data table rows nid. n_hospitals (integer) Number hospitals simulated dataset. time_period (vector) numeric character vector containing data range data years specific dates either format: ("yyyy-mm-dd", "yyyy-mm-dd") (yyyy, yyyy). start date end date (yyyy-01-01 yyyy-12-31) (yyyy, yyyy) date range format provided. Optional cohort provided. cohort (data.frame data.table) Optional, data frame following columns: genc_id (integer): Mock encounter ID number hospital_num (integer): Mock hospital ID number admission_date_time (character): Date time IP admission YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM format discharge_date_time (character): Date time IP discharge YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM format. cohort NULL, nid, n_hospitals, time_period ignored. seed (integer) Optional, number used set seed reproducible results.

title: Generate simulated ipscu data. — dummy_ipscu
text: (data.table) data.table object similar "ipscu" table contains following fields: genc_id (integer): Mock encounter ID; integers starting 1 cohort hospital_num (integer): Mock hospital ID number; integers starting 1 cohort scu_admit_date_time (character): Date time SCU admission YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM format scu_discharge_date_time (character): Date time SCU discharge YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM format icu_flag (logical): Flag specifying whether encounter admitted ICU . refers SCU unit numbers excluding step units 90, 93, 95. scu_unit_number(integer): Code identifying type special care unit patient receives critical care, according DAD abstracting manual 2025-2026 10: Medical Intensive Care Nursing Unit 20: Surgical Intensive Care Nursing Unit 25: Trauma Intensive Care Nursing Unit 30: Combined Medical/Surgical Intensive Care Nursing Unit 35: Burn Intensive Care Nursing Unit 40: Cardiac Intensive Care Nursing Unit Surgery (CCU) 45: Coronary Intensive Care Nursing Unit Medical (CCU) 50: Neonatal Intensive Care Nursing Unit Undifferentiated/General 60: Neurosurgery Intensive Care Nursing Unit 80: Respirology Intensive Care Nursing Unit 90: Step-Medical Unit 93: Combined Medical/Surgical Step-Unit 95: Step-Surgical Unit

title: Generate simulated lab data — dummy_lab_cbc_electrolyte
text: function creates synthetic dataset subset lab tests contained GEMINI "lab" table, seen GEMINI Data Repository Dictionary. function currently focuses simulating two lab tests: hemoglobin sodium, often used identify routine blood work tests complete blood count electrolytes. function return: collection date time, information test type, test code, test result value. long format data table.

title: Generate simulated lab data — dummy_lab_cbc_electrolyte
code: dummy_lab_cbc_electrolyte(   nid = 1000,   n_hospitals = 10,   time_period = c(2015, 2023),   cohort = NULL,   seed = NULL )

title: Generate simulated lab data — dummy_lab_cbc_electrolyte
text: nid (integer) Number unique encounter IDs simulate. data table, ID occurs . ignored cohort provided. n_hospitals (integer) Number hospitals simulated dataset. ignored cohort provided time_period (numeric): Date range data, years specific dates either format: ("yyyy-mm-dd", "yyyy-mm-dd") (yyyy, yyyy). ignored cohort provided. cohort (data.frame data.table) Optional, data frame data table columns: genc_id (integer): Mock encounter ID numbers hospital_num (integer): Mock hospital ID numbers admission_date_time (character): Date time IP admission YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM format discharge_date_time (character): Date time IP discharge YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM format. cohort NULL, nid, n_hospitals, time_period ignored. seed (integer) Optional, number setting seed get reproducible results.

title: Generate simulated lab data — dummy_lab_cbc_electrolyte
text: (data.table) data.table object similar "lab" table contains following fields: genc_id (integer): Mock encounter ID; integers starting 1 seen cohort hospital_num (integer): Mock hospital ID; integers starting 1 seen cohort test_type_mapped_omop (character):	Test name code mapped GEMINI; currently two tests available: 3000963 (hemoglobin) 3019550 (sodium) test_name_raw (character): Test name reported hospital test_code_raw (character): Test code reported hospital result_value (character): Test results collection_date_time (character):	Date time sample collected

what: Generate simulated locality variables data — dummy_locality
title: Generate simulated locality variables data — dummy_locality
text: function creates synthetic dataset subset variables contained GEMINI "locality-variables" table, seen GEMINI Data Repository Dictionary. Specifically, function simulates dissemination area IDs (da21uid) based Canadian census data user-specified set mock encounter hospital IDs. mimic GEMINI data characteristics, majority simulated area IDs drawn Ontario clustered hospital.

title: Generate simulated locality variables data — dummy_locality
code: dummy_locality(   nid = 1000,   n_hospitals = 10,   cohort = NULL,   da21uid = NULL,   seed = NULL )

title: Generate simulated locality variables data — dummy_locality
text: nid (integer) Number unique encounter IDs simulate. data table, ID occurs . optional cohort provided. n_hospitals (integer) Number hospitals simulated dataset. optional cohort provided. cohort (data.frame data.table) Optional, existing data frame data table similar admdad GEMINI least following columns: genc_id (integer): Mock encounter ID, integers starting 1 cohort hospital_num (integer): Mock hospital ID, integers starting 1 cohort cohort provided, nid n_hospital inputs used. da21uid (integer vector) Optional, allows user customize dissemination area ID(s) include output. seed (integer) Optional, number used set seed reproducible results.

title: Generate simulated locality variables data — dummy_locality
text: (data.table) data.table object similar "locality_variables" table contains following fields: genc_id (integer): Mock encounter ID; integers starting 1 cohort provided da21uid (integer): Dissemination area ID based 2021 Canadian census data using PCCF Version 8A

title: Generate simulated locality variables data — dummy_locality
Generate simulated physicians data — dummy_physicians
text: function creates synthetic dataset subset variables contained GEMINI "physicians" table, seen GEMINI Data Repository Dictionary.

title: Generate simulated physicians data — dummy_physicians
code: dummy_physicians(nid = 1000, n_hospitals = 10, cohort = NULL, seed = NULL)

title: Generate simulated physicians data — dummy_physicians
text: nid (integer) Number unique encounter IDs simulate. Optional cohort provided. n_hospitals (integer) Number hospitals simulated dataset. Optional cohort provided. cohort (data.frame data.table) Optional, existing data table data frame similar admdad GEMINI least following columns: genc_id (integer): Mock encounter ID; integers starting 1 hospital_num (integer): Mock hospital ID; integers starting 1 cohort provided, nid n_hospitals inputs used. seed (integer) Optional, number used set seed reproducible results.

title: Generate simulated physicians data — dummy_physicians
text: (data.table) data.table object similar "physicians" table contains following fields: genc_id (integer): Mock encounter ID; integers starting 1 cohort provided hospital_num (integer): Mock hospital ID number; integers starting 1 cohort provided admitting_physician_gim (logical): Whether admitting physician attends general medicine ward discharging_physician_gim (logical): Whether discharging physician attends general medicine ward adm_phy_cpso_mapped (integer): Synthetic mock CPSO number (prefix 'SYN_') admitting physician mrp_cpso_mapped (integer): Synthetic mock CPSO number (prefix 'SYN_') responsible physician (MRP) dis_phy_cpso_mapped (integer): Synthetic mock CPSO number (prefix 'SYN_') discharging physician

title: Generate simulated radiology data — dummy_radiology
text: function creates dummy dataset subset variables contained GEMINI "radiology" table, seen GEMINI Data Repository Dictionary. function simulates modalities used MyPractice OurPractice Reports: CT, MRI, Ultrasound. cover modalities seen actual "radiology" data table.

title: Generate simulated radiology data — dummy_radiology
code: dummy_radiology(   nid = 1000,   n_hospitals = 10,   time_period = c(2015, 2023),   cohort = NULL,   seed = NULL )

title: Generate simulated radiology data — dummy_radiology
text: nid (integer) Number unique encounter IDs simulate. Encounter IDs may repeat simulate multiple radiology tests, resulting data table rows nid. Alternatively, users provide cohort input, function instead simulate radiology data genc_ids user-defined cohort table. n_hospitals (integer) number hospitals simulate. Alternatively, users provide cohort input, function instead simulate radiology data hospital_nums user-defined cohort table time_period (vector) numeric character vector containing data range data years specific dates either format: ("yyyy-mm-dd", "yyyy-mm-dd") (yyyy, yyyy) start date end date (yyyy-01-01 yyyy-12-31) (yyyy, yyyy) date range format provided. Optional cohort provided. cohort (data.frame data.table) Optional, data frame data table following columns: genc_id (integer): Mock encounter ID number hospital_num (integer): Mock hospital ID number admission_date_time (character): Date time IP admission YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM format discharge_date_time (character): Date time IP discharge YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM format. cohort input provided, nid, n_hospitals, time_period ignored. seed (integer) Optional, number used set seed reproducible results.

title: Generate simulated radiology data — dummy_radiology
text: (data.table) data.table object similar contains following fields: genc_id (integer): Mock encounter ID number; integers starting 1 cohort provided hospital_num (integer): Mock hospital ID number; integers starting 1 cohort provided modality_mapped (character): Imaging modality: either MRI, CT, Ultrasound. ordered_date_time (character): date time radiology test ordered performed_date_time (character): date time radiology test performed

title: Generate simulated transfusion data — dummy_transfusion
text: function creates dummy dataset subset variables contained GEMINI "transfusion" table, seen GEMINI Data Repository Dictionary.

title: Generate simulated transfusion data — dummy_transfusion
code: dummy_transfusion(   nid = 1000,   n_hospitals = 10,   time_period = c(2015, 2023),   cohort = NULL,   blood_product_list = NULL,   seed = NULL )

title: Generate simulated transfusion data — dummy_transfusion
text: nid (integer) number unique mock encounter IDs simulate. Encounter IDs may repeat, resulting data table rows nid. Optional cohort provided. n_hospitals (integer) number hospitals simulate, optional cohort provided. time_period (vector) numeric character vector containing data range data years specific dates either format: ("yyyy-mm-dd", "yyyy-mm-dd") (yyyy, yyyy). start date end date (yyyy-01-01 yyyy-12-31) (yyyy, yyyy) date range format provided. used cohort provided. cohort (data.frame data.table) Optional, data frame data table columns: genc_id (integer): Mock encounter ID number hospital_num (integer): Mock hospital ID number admission_date_time (character): Date time IP admission YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM format. discharge_date_time (character): Date time IP discharge YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM format. cohort NULL, nid, n_hospitals, time_period ignored. blood_product_list (character) Either string character vector sample variable blood_product_mapped_omop. Items must real blood product OMOP codes used warning raised. seed (integer) Optional, number used set seed reproducible results.

title: Generate simulated transfusion data — dummy_transfusion
text: (data.table) data.table object similar "transfusion" table following fields: genc_id (integer): Mock encounter ID number; integers starting 1 cohort hospital_num (integer): Mock hospital ID number; integers starting 1 cohort issue_date_time (character): date time transfusion issued, format ("yy-mm-dd hh:mm") blood_product_mapped_omop (character): Blood product name mapped GEMINI following international standard. blood_product_raw (character): Type blood product component transfused reported hospital.

what: Generate a data table with basic inpatient stay information. At the minimum, it will include an encounter and hospital ID, along with other information if cohort is included in the input. — generate_id_hospital
title: Generate a data table with basic inpatient stay information. At the minimum, it will include an encounter and hospital ID, along with other information if cohort is included in the input. — generate_id_hospital
text: function creates data table simulated encounter IDs hospital IDs. creation either based user's desired number encounters unique hospitals, based given set encounter IDs. can used create long format data tables users control average repeat frequency.

title: Generate a data table with basic inpatient stay information. At the minimum, it will include an encounter and hospital ID, along with other information if cohort is included in the input. — generate_id_hospital
code: generate_id_hospital(   nid = 1000,   n_hospitals = 10,   avg_repeats = 1.5,   include_prop = 1,   cohort = NULL,   by_los = FALSE,   seed = NULL )

title: Generate a data table with basic inpatient stay information. At the minimum, it will include an encounter and hospital ID, along with other information if cohort is included in the input. — generate_id_hospital
text: nid (integer) Optional, number unique encounter IDs simulate n_hospitals (integer) Optional, number hospitals simulate assign encounter IDs avg_repeats (numeric) average number repeats per row final data table include_prop (numeric) number 0 1, proportion unique rows cohort include final data table cohort (data.table) Optional, resembling GEMINI "admdad" table build returned data table by_los (logical) Optional, whether assign repeats longer hospital stays . Default FALSE. TRUE, two additional columns required input cohort dataset - admission_date_time discharge_date_time calculating length stay. seed (integer) Optional, number setting seed reproducible results

title: Generate a data table with basic inpatient stay information. At the minimum, it will include an encounter and hospital ID, along with other information if cohort is included in the input. — generate_id_hospital
text: (data.table) data.table object columns cohort, rows excluded /repeated based user specifications. cohort included, following fields: genc_id (integer): Mock encounter number, may repeated multiple rows based avg_repeats hospital_num (integer): Mock hospital ID number

title: Generate a data table with basic inpatient stay information. At the minimum, it will include an encounter and hospital ID, along with other information if cohort is included in the input. — generate_id_hospital
code: sample_cohort <- data.table::data.table(genc_id = 1:100, hospital_num = rep(1:5, each = 20)) generate_id_hospital(cohort = sample_cohort, include_prop = 0.8, avg_repeats = 1.5, by_los = TRUE, seed = 1) #>      genc_id hospital_num admission_date_time discharge_date_time   los #>        <int>        <int>              <POSc>              <POSc> <num> #>   1:      68            4                <NA>                <NA>    NA #>   2:      39            2                <NA>                <NA>    NA #>   3:       1            1                <NA>                <NA>    NA #>   4:      34            2                <NA>                <NA>    NA #>   5:      87            5                <NA>                <NA>    NA #>  ---                                                                    #> 129:      19            1                <NA>                <NA>    NA #> 130:      19            1                <NA>                <NA>    NA #> 131:      19            1                <NA>                <NA>    NA #> 132:      19            1                <NA>                <NA>    NA #> 133:      19            1                <NA>                <NA>    NA generate_id_hospital(nid = 1000, n_hospitals = 10, avg_repeats = 1) #>       genc_id hospital_num #>         <int>        <int> #>    1:       1            4 #>    2:       2            2 #>    3:       3            1 #>    4:       4            7 #>    5:       5            9 #>   ---                      #>  996:     996            9 #>  997:     997            6 #>  998:     998            6 #>  999:     999            6 #> 1000:    1000            1

title: Simulate ICD-10 Diagnosis Codes — sample_icd
text: function simulates ICD-10 diagnosis codes random user specified pattern.

title: Simulate ICD-10 Diagnosis Codes — sample_icd
code: sample_icd(n = 1, source = "comorbidity", dbcon = NULL, pattern = NULL)

title: Simulate ICD-10 Diagnosis Codes — sample_icd
text: n (integer) Number ICD codes simulate. source (string) source ICD coding sample . Default "comorbidity" 2011 version ICD-10 codes implemented R comorbidity package. source icd_lookup, ICD-10-CA codes sampled lookup_icd10_ca_description table GEMINI database, see details GEMINI Data Repository Dictionary. dbcon (DBIConnection) database connection GEMINI database. Required source icd_lookup. pattern (string) valid regex expression specifies desired pattern returned ICD codes matched .

title: Simulate ICD-10 Diagnosis Codes — sample_icd
text: (vector) vector ICD diagnostic codes.

title: Simulate ICD-10 Diagnosis Codes — sample_icd
code: ### Simulate 100 ICD-10 codes based on the 2011 version. if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ sample_icd(100, source = "comorbidity") } # }  ### Simulate 100 ICD-10 codes starting with "C2" or "E10" based on the 2011 version. if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ sample_icd(100, source = "comorbidity", pattern = "^C2|^E10") } # }  ### Simulate 50 ICD-10-CA codes based on codes found in the `lookup_icd10_ca_description` table if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ drv <- dbDriver("PostgreSQL") dbcon <- DBI::dbConnect(drv,   dbname = "db",   host = "domain_name.ca",   port = 1234,   user = getPass("Enter user:"),   password = getPass("password") ) sample_icd(50, source = "icd_lookup", dbcon = dbcon) } # }

what: Sample SCU admission and discharge date times by genc_id. — sample_scu_date_time
title: Sample SCU admission and discharge date times by genc_id. — sample_scu_date_time
text: "ipscu" data table long format data table multiple repeats genc_id. SCU admission discharge date times must sampled way SCU stays occur inpatient admission discharge dates times, patient multiple SCU stays, overlap. helper function dummy_ipscu.

title: Sample SCU admission and discharge date times by genc_id. — sample_scu_date_time
code: sample_scu_date_time(   scu_cohort,   use_ip_dates = TRUE,   start_date = NULL,   end_date = NULL,   seed = NULL )

title: Sample SCU admission and discharge date times by genc_id. — sample_scu_date_time
text: scu_cohort (data.table) dummy data table requiring addition SCU admission discharge date time columns. requires following columns: genc_occurrence (integer): genc_id, numbered appearance data table, .e. 1, 2, 3, ... genc_id (integer): Mock encounter ID; integers starting 1 hospital_num (integer): Mock hospital ID number; integers starting 1 use_ip_dates TRUE, also require following columns: admission_date_time (POSIXct): date time IP admission YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM format discharge_date_time (POSIXct): date time IP discharge YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM format use_ip_dates (logical) Optional, whether table scu_cohort contains information inpatient admission discharge date times. TRUE, function sample SCU data based date times , sample random. start_date (Date) Optional, earliest date range SCU admissions dummy data table. used use_ip_dates TRUE. end_date (Date) Optional, latest date range SCU admissions dummy data table. used use_ip_dates TRUE. seed (integer) Optional, integer setting seed reproducible results.

title: Sample SCU admission and discharge date times by genc_id. — sample_scu_date_time
text: (data.table) copy input, scu_cohort, returned. contain fields, addition : scu_admit_date_time (character): date time admission SCU scu_discharge_date_time (character): date time discharge SCU

code: sample_time_shifted(nrow, xi, omega, alpha, min = 0, max = 48, seed = NULL)

title: Chopped, skewed normal distribution for time variables — sample_time_shifted
text: nrow (integer) number data points sample xi (numeric) center skewed normal distribution omega (numeric) spread skewed normal distribution alpha (numeric) skewness skewed normal distribution min (numeric) Optional, minimum value left truncate value ; default value set 0. max (numeric) Optional, maximum value right truncate value ; default value set 48. seed (integer) Optional, integer setting seed reproducible results.

title: Chopped, skewed normal distribution for time variables — sample_time_shifted
text: numeric vector following specified distribution.

what: Chopped log normal distribution for time variables — sample_time_shifted_lnorm
title: Chopped log normal distribution for time variables — sample_time_shifted_lnorm
text: function samples log normal using rlnorm obtain time day data hours. Values greater 24 subtracted 24 (moved next day) real time variable observed.

title: Chopped log normal distribution for time variables — sample_time_shifted_lnorm
code: sample_time_shifted_lnorm(nrow, meanlog, sdlog, min = 0, max = 48, seed = NULL)

title: Chopped log normal distribution for time variables — sample_time_shifted_lnorm
text: nrow (integer) number data points sample meanlog (numeric) log mean distribution sdlog (numeric) log standard deviation min (numeric) Optional, minimum value left truncate value ; default value set 0. max (numeric) Optional, maximum value right truncate value ; default value set 48. seed (integer) Optional, integer setting seed reproducible results.

title: Chopped log normal distribution for time variables — sample_time_shifted_lnorm
text: numeric vector following specified distribution.

title: Data simulation wrapper function — simulate_data_tables
text: wrapper coordinates table-specific simulation functions generate relational synthetic tables reflect inter-table structure GEMINI data. Users specify tables generate provide shared inputs number encounters, hospitals, time period. function returns list simulated data.tables inter-table relationships handled automatically. Specifically, admdad table generated first provides encounter IDs used primary key subsequent table. tables simulated mirror real-world linkage patterns admdad table GEMINI data. Available tables include: admdad ipscu er erdiagnosis ipdiagnosis locality_variables lab *: currently simulates CBC electrolyte tests radiology *: currently simulates MRI, CT, ultrasound imaging data erintervention *: currently simulates intervention MRI ipintervention *: currently simulates interventions MRI MAID transfusion: transfusion information blood product issue date times physicians See GEMINI Data Repository Dictionary table definitions individual simulation function documentation details.

title: Data simulation wrapper function — simulate_data_tables
code: simulate_data_tables(   tables,   nid = 1000,   n_hospitals = 10,   time_period = c(2015, 2023),   ... )

title: Data simulation wrapper function — simulate_data_tables
text: tables (vector) character vector listing names required data tables nid (integer) number mock encounter IDs simulate. n_hospitals (integer) number mock hospital ID numbers simulate. time_period (vector) numeric character vector containing data range data years specific dates either format: ("yyyy-mm-dd", "yyyy-mm-dd") (yyyy, yyyy). start date end date (yyyy-01-01 yyyy-12-31) (yyyy, yyyy) date range format provided. ... Additional arguments may passed data simulation functions. arguments normally used customize table outputs.

title: Data simulation wrapper function — simulate_data_tables
text: (list) list containing data.table objects, one per entry tables parameter entries valid GEMINI data tables. May include: admdad, ipscu, er, erdiagnosis, ipdiagnosis, locality_variables, lab, radiology, erintervention, ipintervention, transfusion, physicians

title: Data simulation wrapper function — simulate_data_tables
text: Wrapper function calls data simulation functions create synthetic, customizable database reflects inter-table relations GEMINI database.
